description: Query and export photos from macOS Photos library

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use to configure authentication credentials. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate. Use --credential-type/-c to authenticate a specific credential type.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p to target a specific profile.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use to check current authentication status. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials are valid. Use --verbose/-v for detailed output. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list all profiles and see which is the default. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific profile. NAME is a required positional arg.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new profile. NAME is a required positional arg.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use to switch the active profile. NAME is a required positional arg.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use to permanently delete a profile. NAME is a required positional arg. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management -- list, get, create, set default, and delete profiles. Standard profile group for multi-account support.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management for Photos app access. Standard auth group for profile-based credential management. Photos app itself doesn't require OAuth but profiles are supported for consistency.

list.help: List photos from the Photos library. By default returns JSON with photo metadata. Use --table for tabular output.
list.usage_instructions: Use to list photos with metadata from the Photos library. Filter by date range with --from and --to (YYYY-MM-DD format). Filter by album with --album/-a. Default limit is 100; adjust with --limit/-l. Use --videos/-v to include videos. Use --hidden to include hidden photos. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (e.g., 'is_favorite:eq:true'). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

get.help: Get details for a single photo by UUID.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get detailed metadata for a specific photo. UUID is a required positional arg. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

download.help: Download/export photos to a destination folder. This command exports photos via Photos.app, which automatically handles downloading from iCloud if the photos are not stored locally.
download.usage_instructions: Use to export photos from the Photos library to a local folder. DESTINATION is a required positional arg (the export folder). Filter with --album/-a, --from/-f, --to/-t date range. Default limit is 100; adjust with --limit/-l. Use --videos/-v to include videos. Automatically downloads iCloud photos.

count.help: Show total number of photos in the library.
count.usage_instructions: Use to get the total photo count in the library. No arguments or options needed.

photos.usage_instructions: Photo operations -- list photos with metadata, get details for a specific photo, download/export photos to a local folder, and count total photos. Use `photos list` with date range and album filters. Downloads handle iCloud photos automatically.

open.help: Open the Photos app to the Albums view. Launches Photos.app and navigates to My Albums in the sidebar.
open.usage_instructions: Use to open Photos.app directly to the Albums view. No arguments needed.

list.help: List all albums in the Photos library. By default shows only user-created albums. Use --system to include system albums (imports, sync progress, etc.).
list.usage_instructions: Use to list all albums. Shows user-created albums by default; use --system/-s to include system albums. Default limit is 100. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (e.g., 'title:like:%Vacation%'). Use --exclude-folder to hide albums in a specific folder. Use --properties/-p to select fields like uuid, title, photo_count.

get.help: Get details for a single album by name or UUID.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific album. IDENTIFIER is a required positional arg -- can be album name or UUID. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

create.help: Create a new album in the Photos library. Creates an empty album with the specified name. Use Photos.app to add photos to the album.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new empty album. NAME is a required positional arg. Use `albums add-photos` to add photos to the album after creation.

delete.help: Delete an album from the Photos library. This only deletes the album itself, not the photos in it. The photos will remain in your library.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Name of the album to delete

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete an album (photos inside are preserved). NAME is a required positional arg. Use --yes/-y to skip confirmation. Only deletes the album container, not the photos.

move.help: Move an album into a folder. Moves the specified album into the target folder. The folder is auto-created if it doesn't exist. Photos in the album are preserved.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Name of the album to move

move.usage_instructions: Use to organize albums into folders. NAME is a required positional arg. --folder/-f is required to specify the target folder (auto-created if missing). Use --yes/-y to skip confirmation.

enhance.help: Auto-enhance all photos in an album. Applies the built-in Auto Enhance (Cmd+E) to each photo in the album sequentially via UI automation. Your computer will be unusable during the batch — Photos.app must stay in the foreground. Note: Auto-enhance is a toggle. Running on already-enhanced photos will REMOVE the enhancement. Requires Accessibility permissions for System Events.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Album name to enhance photos in

enhance.usage_instructions: Use to auto-enhance all photos in an album via UI automation (Cmd+E). NAME is a required positional arg. WARNING: This blocks the computer during execution -- Photos.app must stay in foreground. Auto-enhance is a toggle, so running on already-enhanced photos removes enhancement. Default limit is 100; adjust with --limit/-l. Use --delay/-d to adjust seconds between UI steps (default 1.5). Use --yes/-y to skip confirmation.

add-photos.help: Add photos to an existing album. Adds one or more photos (by UUID) to the specified album. Photo UUIDs can be obtained from 'photos-app photos list'.
add-photos.usage_instructions: Use to add photos to an album by UUID. --album/-a is required (album name). --photo-ids/-p is required (comma-separated UUIDs). Get photo UUIDs from `photos-app photos list`.

albums.usage_instructions: Album management -- list, get, create, delete, move, enhance, and add photos to albums. Use `albums list` to see all albums. Use `albums enhance` to auto-enhance all photos in an album via UI automation (blocks the computer). Use `albums add-photos` to add photos by UUID.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `photos-app cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `photos-app cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for querying and exporting photos from the macOS Photos library. Commands follow `photos-app <group> <action>` pattern. Supports --table for table output, --filter/-f for field-based filtering, and --properties/-p for field selection. Works directly with the macOS Photos.app SQLite database and AppleScript automation. No API key needed.
